one_liner: FNMA down 5.5% with no grade, no DCF, and no clear catalyst — pure speculative conservatorship-exit play; the Wyckoff Accumulation A-B signals are real but binary political/regulatory risk dominates.

news_catalyst.headline: Fannie Mae Down 5.5% — No Specific News; Conservatorship Exit Timeline Uncertainty
news_catalyst.summary: FNMA is down 5.5% with no specific news catalyst. This is a pure speculative play on the potential exit from government conservatorship — a regulatory event that has been 'imminent' for years without materializing. The stock has no DCF (no conventional valuation applies) and no grade. Wyckoff shows Accumulation Phase A-B with 2/5 signals and bullish MACD, but the regulatory binary makes technical analysis of limited value.

technical.summary: RSI neutral at 51, MACD bullish crossover, but volume drying up. Wyckoff shows Accumulation A-B. Technical setup is modestly positive but the binary regulatory catalyst makes this a speculation, not an investment. Support at $6.04 ($0.84 below) is close.

key_strengths: If privatized, enormous mortgage guarantee franchise value unlocked
key_concerns: Binary conservatorship exit risk — political not fundamental
No conventional financial metrics applicable under current structure
fundamentals.summary: Not gradeable under conventional metrics. FNMA is a regulatory arbitrage play. Position sizing should reflect the binary nature of the outcome.

risks: Binary regulatory risk — conservatorship exit could be delayed indefinitely
Down 5.5% with no news — suggests forced selling or loss of confidence
No grade, no DCF — purely speculative
